title: Harness-level outcome-rate throttle for high-confidence fast failures
description: Add a sliding-window failure-rate tracker to the harness dispatch loop in core.py. Track (task_type, success/failure, timestamp) for the last N completions. If the failure rate within a recent window exceeds a threshold while dispatch frequency is also high, pause dispatch for that task type for a cooldown period. This is orthogonal to any agent-side confidence reporting.
rationale: The reading note identifies a blind spot: agent self-monitoring can detect low-confidence slow decisions, but may fail to detect high-confidence fast errors (overfitting to local patterns). The harness cannot see internal confidence, but it CAN observe behavioral outcomes — success/failure rates over time. This is the complementary information the note says each layer has that the other lacks. By adding outcome-rate tracking at the harness layer, Mira gains detection of exactly the failure mode that agent self-reporting would miss: rapid task dispatch with a rising failure rate, which is the signature of overfit high-confidence errors.
diff_description: In config.py, add: HARNESS_OUTCOME_WINDOW = 10 (last N task completions per type to track), HARNESS_FAILURE_RATE_THRESHOLD = 0.5 (halt if >50% of recent completions failed), HARNESS_FAILURE_COOLDOWN_SECONDS = 300 (5-minute pause before retrying that task type). In core.py, maintain a deque per task_type in a module-level dict (e.g. _outcome_history: dict[str, deque[bool]]). After each task worker returns, append success/failure to the deque. At dispatch time (before launching a task), check if len(history) >= HARNESS_OUTCOME_WINDOW and failure_rate > threshold — if so, skip dispatch and log a warning: 'harness throttle: [task_type] failure rate [X]% over last [N] runs, cooling down [300]s'. Reset the cooldown timer on each new failure within the window.
